📖  Grammar — Past Continuous — was/were + -ing (background action in a story)
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The PAST CONTINUOUS describes a BACKGROUND ACTION or ONGOING SITUATION at a specific time in the past. Form: WAS/WERE + verb-ING. It is often used with PAST SIMPLE to show: 1) A longer background action interrupted by a shorter one: 'I WAS SLEEPING (continuous) when the phone RANG (past simple).' 2) Two parallel actions happening at the same time: 'While she WAS COOKING, he WAS WATCHING TV.' Signal words: when, while, as, at that moment, at 7pm, all morning.

📖  Grammar — Past Simple vs Past Continuous — when and while
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

KEY RULE: PAST CONTINUOUS + WHEN + PAST SIMPLE = a longer action interrupted by a shorter one. 'I WAS SLEEPING (long) WHEN the phone RANG (short, interrupted).' PAST SIMPLE + WHILE + PAST CONTINUOUS = the same idea from a different angle. 'The phone RANG (simple) WHILE I WAS SLEEPING (continuous).' Both mean the same! WHEN + Past Simple = two completed actions in sequence: 'WHEN she arrived, I LEFT.' (first she arrived, then I left).

ГОЛ ДҮРЭМ: PAST CONTINUOUS + WHEN + PAST SIMPLE = урт үйлдлийг богино үйлдэл таслана. 'I WAS SLEEPING WHEN the phone RANG.' PAST SIMPLE + WHILE + PAST CONTINUOUS = ижил санаа өөр өнцгөөс. 'The phone RANG WHILE I WAS SLEEPING.' Хоёул ижил утгатай! WHEN + Past Simple + Past Simple = дараалсан хоёр дууссан үйлдэл: 'WHEN she arrived, I LEFT.' (эхлээд тэр ирсэн, дараа нь би явсан).

It rang while I was sleeping.
It didn't ring while I was sleeping.

When she left, I didn't go.

What happened as you were leaving?
WHILE = two actions at same time · WHEN = one interrupts OR two in sequence

WHEN — нэг үйлдэл нөгөөг нь таслах ЭСВЭЛ дараалсан хоёр үйлдэл

• PS + WHILE + PC = same but other way
• WHEN + PS + PS = two events in sequence

📖  Grammar — Present Perfect Continuous — have/has been + -ing (ongoing action up to now)
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The PRESENT PERFECT CONTINUOUS (have/has + been + verb-ING) describes an action that STARTED in the past and is STILL CONTINUING now, or has VERY RECENTLY stopped. Focus is on the DURATION of the action. 'I have been studying English for 63 days.' (started 63 days ago, still studying NOW). Compare: Present Perfect Simple = completed result: 'I have studied Chapter 5.' (it's finished). Present Perfect Continuous = ongoing process: 'I have been studying Chapter 5.' (still doing it).

PRESENT PERFECT CONTINUOUS (have/has + been + үйл үг+ING) нь өнгөрсөнд эхэлж ОДОО ХҮРТЭЛ ҮРГЭЛЖИЛЖ байгаа эсвэл МАШ САЯХАН ДУУССАН үйлдлийг тайлбарладаг. Хугацаанд анхаарлаа хандуулдаг. 'I have been studying English for 63 days.' (63 өдрийн өмнө эхэлсэн, ОДОО ч гэсэн суралцаж байна). Харьцуул: Present Perfect Simple = дууссан үр дүн. Present Perfect Continuous = үргэлжилж буй процесс.

📖  Grammar — Present Perfect Continuous for duration — How long have you been...?
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

'HOW LONG HAVE YOU BEEN + -ING?' is the key question for Present Perfect Continuous. It asks about the DURATION of an ongoing action. 'How long have you been learning English?' 'I have been learning for 64 days / since last month.' We use this for: 1) Actions that are STILL HAPPENING: 'She has been teaching here for 10 years.' 2) Actions that JUST STOPPED with a visible result: 'Your eyes are red — have you been crying?' Key: Perfect Continuous emphasises the process and duration, not just the result.

'HOW LONG HAVE YOU BEEN + -ING?' нь Present Perfect Continuous-ийн гол асуулт. Үргэлжилж байсан үйлдлийн ХУГАЦААГ асуудаг. 'How long have you been learning English?' 'I have been learning for 64 days / since last month.' Хэрэглэх тохиолдол: 1) ОДОО Ч ГЭСЭН ҮРГЭЛЖИЛЖ БАЙГАА үйлдэл. 2) САЯХАН ДУУССАН үр дүн харагдаж байгаа үйлдэл: 'Your eyes are red — have you been crying?' Процесс, хугацааг онцолдог.

📖  Grammar — Third Conditional — If I had studied, I would have passed
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The THIRD CONDITIONAL talks about IMAGINARY situations in the PAST — things that did NOT happen. We use it for REGRETS and SPECULATION about the past. Structure: IF + HAD + past participle, WOULD HAVE + past participle. 'If I HAD STUDIED harder, I WOULD HAVE PASSED the exam.' (I didn't study → I didn't pass → past regret). Compare: 1st Conditional = real future. 2nd Conditional = unreal present. 3rd Conditional = unreal PAST (it already happened — or didn't happen!).

THIRD CONDITIONAL нь ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГ дахь БОДИТ БУС нөхцлийн тухай ярьдаг — болоогүй зүйлс. ХАРАМСАЛ, ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН ТААМАГЛАЛД хэрэглэнэ. Хэлбэр: IF + HAD + past participle, WOULD HAVE + past participle. 'If I HAD STUDIED, I WOULD HAVE PASSED.' (Суралцаагүй → Тэнцээгүй → өнгөрсөн харамсал). Харьцуул: 1st = бодит ирээдүй. 2nd = бодит бус одоо. 3rd = бодит бус ӨНГӨРСӨН!

📖  Grammar — Mixed Conditionals — combining 2nd and 3rd conditionals
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

MIXED CONDITIONALS combine two different time frames. Type 1 (Past→Present): IF + HAD + past participle (past), WOULD + base verb (present). 'If I HAD STUDIED harder (I didn't — past), I WOULD BE fluent now (I'm not — present).' Type 2 (Present→Past): IF + Past Simple (present), WOULD HAVE + past participle (past). 'If I WERE smarter (I'm not — present), I WOULD HAVE SOLVED that problem (past).' Mixed conditionals show how past actions affect the present and vice versa.

ХОЛИМОГ НӨХЦӨЛТ ӨГҮҮЛБЭР нь хоёр өөр цагийн хүрээг хослуулдаг. 1-р төрөл (Өнгөрсөн→Одоо): IF + HAD + past participle (өнгөрсөн), WOULD + үндсэн үйл үг (одоо). 'If I HAD STUDIED harder, I WOULD BE fluent now.' 2-р төрөл (Одоо→Өнгөрсөн): IF + Past Simple (одоо), WOULD HAVE + past participle (өнгөрсөн). 'If I WERE smarter, I WOULD HAVE SOLVED it.' Холимог нөхцөлт нь өнгөрсөн ба одоогийн хоорондын уялдааг харуулдаг.

Type 2: If I WERE (present) → I WOULD HAVE (past) — одоогийн нөхцөл, өнгөрсөн үр дүн

• Shows links between past and present

Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

MODALS OF DEDUCTION express how CERTAIN we are about something. PRESENT deductions: MUST BE = I am almost certain (positive). CAN'T BE = I am almost certain (negative/impossible). MIGHT/MAY/COULD BE = I think it's possible (50% or less). PAST deductions: MUST HAVE + past participle = almost certain about past. CAN'T HAVE + past participle = impossible in the past. MIGHT HAVE + past participle = possible in the past. Scale: must be (95%) > may/might be (50%) > can't be (0%).

ТААМАГЛАЛЫН MODAL ҮЙЛҮГС нь ямар нэгэн зүйлийн тухай ХЭР ИТГЭЛТЭЙ байгааг илэрхийлдэг. ОДООГИЙН таамаглал: MUST BE = бараг тодорхой (эерэг). CAN'T BE = бараг тодорхой (сөрөг). MIGHT/MAY/COULD BE = боломжтой гэж бодно (50% эсвэл бага). ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН таамаглал: MUST HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд бараг тодорхой. CAN'T HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд боломжгүй. MIGHT HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд боломжтой байсан. Масштаб: must be (95%) > may/might be (50%) > can't be (0%).

📖  Grammar — Might / Could / Should — future predictions with uncertainty
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

For FUTURE UNCERTAINTY, we use modal verbs instead of 'will': MIGHT = small possibility, I'm not very sure (40-60%): 'It might rain.' MAY = slightly more formal, similar to might (40-60%): 'She may arrive late.' COULD = possibility or suggestion (40-60%): 'There could be delays.' SHOULD = expectation, things going as expected (80%): 'The package should arrive tomorrow.' These modals help you sound more natural and less absolute — the future is rarely certain!

ИРЭЭДҮЙН ТОДОРХОЙГҮЙ байдалд 'will'-ийн оронд modal үйлүгс хэрэглэнэ: MIGHT = бага зэрэг боломж, тийм баттай биш (40-60%): 'It might rain.' MAY = нэг хэмжээгээр бүдүүвч might-тэй төстэй (40-60%). COULD = боломж эсвэл санал (40-60%): 'There could be delays.' SHOULD = хүлээлт, зүйл нь зохистой явагдах (80%): 'It should arrive tomorrow.' Эдгээр modal нь ирээдүй нь үргэлж тодорхойгүй гэсэн нарийн утгыг илэрхийлдэг.
Add 'probably' to boost: 'It will probably rain.' (~80%)

FORMAL ENGLISH uses specific vocabulary and structures. Key contrasts: Informal → Formal: 'Hi' → 'Dear Sir/Madam'; 'I want' → 'I would like'; 'Can you?' → 'Could you please?'; 'Thanks' → 'Thank you very much'; 'Sorry' → 'I apologise'; 'Get' → 'obtain/receive'; 'Ask' → 'enquire'; 'About' → 'regarding'. 📖  Grammar — Discourse markers — however, although, despite, nevertheless
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

DISCOURSE MARKERS connect ideas and show logical relationships between sentences. CONTRAST markers: However / Nevertheless / That said (sentence connector + comma). Although / Even though / Whereas (within a sentence). Despite / In spite of (+ noun/-ing, NOT + clause). 'Despite the rain...' (correct) vs 'Despite it rained' (wrong). CAUSE/RESULT: Therefore / Consequently / As a result. CONCESSION: Admittedly / To be fair / On the other hand. These markers transform choppy sentences into flowing, sophisticated B1+ writing.

ХОЛБООС ҮГС нь санааг холбож логик харилцааг харуулдаг. ЭСЭРГҮҮЦЛИЙН холбоос: However / Nevertheless / That said (өгүүлбэр холбогч + таслал). Although / Even though / Whereas (өгүүлбэрийн дотор). Despite / In spite of (+ нэр / -ing, өгүүлбэр биш!). 'Despite the rain...' (зөв) vs 'Despite it rained' (буруу). ШАЛТГААН/ҮР ДҮН: Therefore / Consequently / As a result. БУУЛТ: Admittedly / To be fair / On the other hand.
I agree. However, I have concerns.
Although it's hard, I keep trying.
Despite the rain, we went out.
Despite / In spite of + NOUN or -ING (NOT + clause!)
Despite the rain ✅ · Despite it rained ❌ · Despite raining ✅
However / Nevertheless = start of a NEW sentence (+ comma)
Despite + нэр үг / -ing (өгүүлбэр биш!): Despite the rain / Despite raining
However, Nevertheless — шинэ өгүүлбэрийн эхэнд таслалтай хэрэглэнэ

HOWEVER, what are the benefits? · ALTHOUGH it is difficult, CAN you continue? · DESPITE the challenges, WHAT have you achieved?
• Although / Even though = within a sentence
• Despite / In spite of + noun or -ing (not clause!)
• On the other hand = show another perspective
❌ Despite it rained, we went out.  ✅ Despite THE RAIN, we went out. (despite + noun!)
❌ Although, she passed the test.  ✅ Although she didn't study, she passed. (although needs two clauses!)

❌ In spite of she was nervous, she spoke.  ✅ In spite of BEING nervous, she spoke. (-ing after in spite of!)

📖  Grammar — Wish + Past Simple — I wish I could fly / I wish I had more time
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

WISH + PAST SIMPLE expresses a PRESENT WISH about something that is NOT true or NOT possible NOW. 'I wish I WERE taller.' (I am not tall — present reality.) 'I wish I COULD fly.' (I cannot fly — present reality.) 'I wish I HAD more time.' (I don't have enough time now.) KEY: Always use PAST SIMPLE or COULD (not present simple) after 'wish' for present situations. 'I wish I were' NOT 'I wish I am'. Compare: WISH + WOULD = wishing for a change in someone's behaviour: 'I wish she WOULD stop talking.'

(Би нисч чадахгүй). 'I wish I HAD more time.' (Надад хангалтттай цаг байхгүй). ЧУХАЛ: 'wish'-ийн дараа PAST SIMPLE эсвэл COULD (Present Simple биш!). 'I wish I were' — 'I wish I am' биш. WISH + WOULD = хэн нэгний зан авирыг өөрчлөхийг хүсэх.

📖  Grammar — Indirect questions — Could you tell me where...? / Do you know if...?
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

INDIRECT QUESTIONS are more polite than direct questions. They use statement word order (not question word order). Direct: 'Where IS the hotel?' → Indirect: 'Could you tell me where the hotel IS?' (normal order, no inversion). Direct: 'Is the tour available?' → Indirect: 'Do you know IF the tour is available?' (use if/whether for yes/no questions). Key: NO question mark in indirect questions that use could/would. The sentence ends with a period if it is a polite statement, or ? if it is genuinely a question.

ШУУД БУС АСУУЛТ нь шууд асуултаас илүү зохистой. Тэд өгүүлбэрийн үгийн дарааллыг (асуултын биш) хэрэглэдэг. Шууд: 'Where IS the hotel?' → Шууд бус: 'Could you tell me where the hotel IS?' (хэвийн дараалал, эргүүлэлт байхгүй). Тийм/Үгүй асуултад: 'Do you know IF the tour is available?' (if/whether хэрэглэнэ). ЧУХАЛ: Шууд бус асуулт дахь үгийн дараалал — асуулт биш, өгүүлбэрийн дараалал!

• Could you tell me what time it IS? (not 'is it'!)

📖  Grammar — Gerunds vs Infinitives — enjoy doing / want to do
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

GERUNDS (verb + -ing) are used after: enjoy, avoid, finish, keep, mind, suggest, consider, practise, can't help, give up, miss, deny. INFINITIVES (to + base verb) are used after: want, hope, plan, decide, manage, agree, refuse, offer, fail, need, learn, expect. MEANING CHANGE with some verbs: 'stop SMOKING' (quit) vs 'stop TO SMOKE' (pause in order to). 'remember POSTING' (past action) vs 'remember TO POST' (future task). 'try READING aloud' (experiment) vs 'try TO READ' (make an effort).

ГЕРУНД (үйл үг + -ing) — дараах үйл үгийн дараа: enjoy, avoid, finish, keep, mind, suggest, practise, give up, miss. ИНФИНИТИВ (to + үндсэн үйл үг) — дараах үйл үгийн дараа: want, hope, plan, decide, manage, agree, refuse, fail, need, learn, expect. УТГЫН ӨӨРЧЛӨЛТ: 'stop SMOKING' (байнга болих) vs 'stop TO SMOKE' (хийхийн тулд зогсох). 'remember POSTING' (өнгөрсөн) vs 'remember TO POST' (ирээдүй). 'try READING' (туршилт) vs 'try TO READ' (хичээх).
Did he stop to buy water?
GERUND verbs: enjoy / avoid / finish / keep / mind / miss / give up / practise
INFINITIVE verbs: want / hope / plan / decide / manage / agree / refuse / fail
MEANING CHANGE: stop -ing (quit) · stop to (pause) · remember -ing (past) · remember to (future)
ГЕРУНД: enjoy / avoid / finish / keep / mind — эдгээрийн дараа -ING
ИНФИНИТИВ: want / hope / plan / decide — эдгээрийн дараа TO
УТГА ӨӨРЧЛӨГДДӨГ: stop + -ing vs stop + to!
I AVOID EATING junk food. I STOPPED SMOKING last year. I DON'T WANT TO go. She REFUSED TO help.
Do you ENJOY STUDYING? Do you WANT TO travel? Did he STOP SMOKING? Do you REMEMBER SEEING her?
• stop + to = pause in order to do
❌ I enjoy to study English.  ✅ I enjoy STUDYING English. (enjoy + gerund!)
❌ She wants studying abroad.  ✅ She wants TO STUDY abroad. (want + infinitive!)
❌ He stopped to smoke. (meaning: he paused to have a cigarette!)  ✅ He stopped SMOKING. (if he quit!)
❌ Remember posting the letter! (means you already did it!)  ✅ Remember TO POST the letter! (future reminder!)
3. He stopped _____ last year. (smoke → gerund = quit)

CAUSATIVE HAVE: 'Have/get + object + past participle.' Used when YOU ARRANGE for someone ELSE to do something for you. You don't do it yourself. 'I had my hair CUT.' (A hairdresser cut it.) Time: Past = had + obj + p.p. · Present = have/am having + obj + p.p. · Future = will have + obj + p.p. GET + obj + p.p. is more informal and means the same thing.

📖  Grammar — Reported questions — She asked where I lived / He wanted to know if...
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

REPORTED QUESTIONS change word order from question order to statement order, and shift tenses back. WH-QUESTIONS: Direct: 'Where DO you LIVE?' → Reported: She asked WHERE I LIVED. (no inversion! statement order) YES/NO QUESTIONS: Direct: 'ARE you guilty?' → Reported: He asked IF/WHETHER I WAS guilty. (no question mark if it's a statement) Tense shifts: do/does → did, am/is → was, are → were, will → would, can → could, have → had. Verb to introduce: asked, wanted to know, enquired, wondered.

ТАЙЛАГДСАН АСУУЛТ нь асуулт дарааллаас өгүүлбэр дараалалд шилжиж, цаг ухарна. WH-АСУУЛТ: Шууд: 'Where DO you LIVE?' → Тайлагдсан: She asked WHERE I LIVED. (эргүүлэлт байхгүй! өгүүлбэрийн дараалал). ТИЙМ/ҮГҮЙ АСУУЛТ: Шууд: 'ARE you guilty?' → Тайлагдсан: He asked IF/WHETHER I WAS guilty. Цагийн өөрчлөлт: do → did, am/is → was, will → would, can → could, have → had.
He asked if I was guilty.
She asked if I WOULD help.
Reported question = ask/want to know/enquire + WH-word + STATEMENT ORDER
'Where is he?' → She asked WHERE HE WAS. (not 'where was he'!)
'Is she guilty?' → He asked IF SHE WAS guilty.
Тайлагдсан асуулт = ask + WH-word + ӨГҮҮЛБЭРИЙН ДАРААЛАЛ
Shе asked where HE WAS (he was — эргүүлэлт байхгүй!)
He asked IF she WAS guilty (if + subject + verb)
She asked why I HAD NOT been there. He asked whether I WAS NOT going. The judge asked if the defendant DID NOT have an alibi.
She asked WHERE I LIVED. He wanted to know IF she WAS guilty. The officer enquired WHETHER anyone HAD seen the suspect.
• Reported Q = ask + WH-word + statement order
• No inversion! 'where he was' NOT 'where was he'
• Same tense shifts as reported statements
• Verb: asked / wanted to know / enquired / wondered

❌ She asked what time is it.  ✅ She asked what time IT WAS. (tense shift + no inversion!)

📖  Grammar — Past Perfect — I had already eaten when she arrived / He had studied before the exam
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The PAST PERFECT (had + past participle) shows which of two past events happened FIRST. 'When she ARRIVED (past simple — 2nd event), I HAD ALREADY EATEN (past perfect — 1st event).' Key words: already, just, never, before, after, by the time, when. Compare: Past Simple = a completed past action. Past Perfect = the EARLIER of two past actions. Pattern for stories: Past Perfect (background/earlier) → Past Simple (main story event). 'He HAD STUDIED for months before the exam. On the day, he FELT confident.'

PAST PERFECT (had + past participle) нь хоёр өнгөрсөн үйлдлийн аль нь ЭХЛЭЭД болсныг харуулдаг. 'When she ARRIVED (2-р үйлдэл), I HAD ALREADY EATEN (1-р үйлдэл).' Гол үгс: already, just, never, before, after, by the time, when. Харьцуул: Past Simple = дууссан өнгөрсөн үйлдэл. Past Perfect = хоёр өнгөрсөн үйлдлийн ЭРТНИЙ нь. Түүх өгүүлэхэд: Past Perfect (дэвсгэр/эрт) → Past Simple (гол цэг).

I had already left by then.
He had just left when I called.
By the time I arrived, it had ended.

She had never seen snow before.

hadn't = had not · Had you eaten? (асуулт хэлбэр)

• by the time + PS → PP had already happened
• had already = done before the 2nd event
• had just = done very recently before 2nd event

📖  Grammar — Expressing possibility — It is likely that / It is probable that / There is a chance that
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

EXPRESSING POSSIBILITY formally: 'IT IS LIKELY THAT + clause' (high probability, ~80%). 'IT IS PROBABLE THAT + clause' (similar to likely, formal). 'THERE IS A CHANCE THAT + clause' (moderate possibility, ~50%). 'IT IS POSSIBLE THAT + clause' (lower certainty). 'IT IS UNLIKELY THAT + clause' (low probability). These are used in formal interviews, reports, and professional discussions. Compare with informal: 'Maybe I'll get the job' → Formal: 'It is likely that I will be offered the position.'

БОЛОМЖИЙГ АЛБАН ЁСООР илэрхийлэх: 'IT IS LIKELY THAT + өгүүлбэр' (~80% магадлал). 'IT IS PROBABLE THAT + өгүүлбэр' (likely-тэй ижил, бүдүүвч). 'THERE IS A CHANCE THAT + өгүүлбэр' (~50% боломж). 'IT IS UNLIKELY THAT + өгүүлбэр' (бага магадлал). Эдгээрийг ажлын ярилцлага, тайлан, мэргэжлийн яриа хэлэлцэлд хэрэглэнэ. Харьцуул: 'Maybe I'll get it' → Албан: 'It is likely that I will be offered the position.'

📖  Grammar — Academic vocabulary + formal tone — essays and presentations
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

ACADEMIC ENGLISH uses specific vocabulary and structures. Key academic phrases: INTRODUCTION: 'This essay argues that...' / 'This paper examines...' / 'The aim of this essay is to...' ADDING POINTS: 'Furthermore / In addition / Moreover...' SHOWING RESULT: 'Consequently / Therefore / As a result...' SHOWING EVIDENCE: 'The evidence suggests...' / 'This demonstrates...' / 'Studies show...' CONCLUSION: 'In conclusion...' / 'To sum up...' / 'This analysis has shown...' Avoid: 'I think', 'I believe', 'This shows', 'Also', 'And', 'But' → Use formal alternatives.

АКАДЕМИЙН АНГЛИ нь тусгай үгс ба бүтэц хэрэглэдэг. Гол академийн хэллэгүүд: ОРШИЛ: 'This essay argues that...' / 'This paper examines...' НЭМЭЛТийн: 'Furthermore / In addition / Moreover...' ҮР ДҮН: 'Consequently / Therefore / As a result...' НОТОЛГОО: 'The evidence suggests...' / 'This demonstrates...' ДҮГНЭЛТ: 'In conclusion...' / 'To sum up...' Зайлсхийх: 'I think', 'I believe', 'This shows', 'Also', 'And', 'But' → Бүдүүвч хувилбар хэрэглэ.
This essay argues that English is vital.
The evidence suggests a clear link.
Academic writing: Do NOT use 'I think', 'I believe', 'kind of', 'a lot'. Use: 'Evidence suggests', 'This demonstrates', 'significant', 'substantial'.
Academic: WHAT does the evidence SUGGEST? WHAT does this DEMONSTRATE? WHAT can we CONCLUDE from this data?
• Add points: Furthermore / Moreover / In addition
• Show evidence: The evidence suggests / demonstrates
• Conclude: In conclusion / To sum up
• Avoid: I think, shows, also, but, a lot
❌ I think English is important.  ✅ The evidence SUGGESTS that English is crucial. (academic!)
❌ Also, English is useful.  ✅ FURTHERMORE, English demonstrates significant utility.
❌ This shows that people need English.  ✅ This DEMONSTRATES that English proficiency is essential.
❌ In conclusion, it's a big deal.  ✅ In conclusion, English represents a CONSIDERABLE advantage.

Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

NOUN PHRASES add detailed description before or after a noun. In English, adjectives come BEFORE the noun (unlike Mongolian). Complex noun phrases: 'a RAPIDLY GROWING technology company.' Pattern: (adverb + adjective) + (adjective) + noun. Multiple adjectives: opinion → size → age → shape → colour → origin → material → noun. Examples: 'a well-established international financial corporation.' 'a highly competitive rapidly expanding market.' In formal and business writing, noun phrases allow precise description without extra sentences.

Хэлбэр: (тодотгол + тэмдэг нэр) + (тэмдэг нэр) + нэр үг. Олон тэмдэг нэр: санаа → хэмжээ → нас → хэлбэр → өнгө → гарал → материал → нэр үг. Бизнес, албан бичигт нэрийн хэллэг нэмэлт өгүүлбэргүйгээр нарийвчлалтай тайлбар хийх боломж олгодог.

📖  Grammar — Concession clauses — although, even though, while, whereas, in spite of the fact that
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

CONCESSION CLAUSES show contrast or unexpected results despite a stated condition. WITHIN A SENTENCE: Although / Even though / While / Whereas + clause. 'Although the policy is expensive, it has improved outcomes.' 'Even though protests occurred, the law was passed.' 'While some support the change, others strongly oppose it.' + FULL CLAUSE (subject + verb). CLAUSE STARTERS: 'Despite the fact that / In spite of the fact that + clause' (more formal/emphatic). 'Granted that it has flaws, the policy is still necessary.'

БУУЛТЫН ХЭСГҮҮД нь тогтсон нөхцлийг үл харгалзан харьцуулсан тохиолдол буюу гэнэтийн үр дүнг харуулдаг. ӨГҮҮЛБЭРИЙН ДОТОР: Although / Even though / While / Whereas + өгүүлбэр. 'Although the policy is expensive, it has improved outcomes.' 'While some support it, others oppose it.' + БҮТЭН ӨГҮҮЛБЭР (субьект + үйл үг). ХЭЛБЭР: 'Despite the fact that / In spite of the fact that + өгүүлбэр' (бүдүүвч/хүчтэй). 'Granted that it has flaws, the policy is still necessary.'
Although it is costly, it works.
Even though it was hard, I succeeded.
Despite the fact that it costs more, demand is high.
Although / Even though / While + FULL CLAUSE (subject + verb!)
Despite the fact that + FULL CLAUSE (NOT despite + noun!)
Whereas = shows direct contrast between two situations
Although / Even though / While + БҮТЭН ӨГҮҮЛБЭР (subject + verb!)
Despite the fact that + БҮТЭН ӨГҮҮЛБЭР (noun биш!)
Whereas = хоёр нөхцлийн шууд эсрэг байдал
Although it does NOT work perfectly, it is still useful. Even though she did NOT agree, she voted for it. Despite the fact that costs have NOT fallen, demand remains high.
ALTHOUGH it has flaws, IS it still worth doing? EVEN THOUGH it is controversial, SHOULD it be implemented? WHEREAS some benefit, HOW do we help those who don't?
• Despite the fact that + FULL clause (not noun!)
❌ Although the policy, it works.  ✅ Although THE POLICY IS EXPENSIVE, it works. (need full clause!)
❌ Even though expensive, people buy it.  ✅ Even though IT IS expensive, people buy it.
❌ Despite the fact poverty, they succeed.  ✅ Despite the fact THAT poverty exists, they succeed.
❌ Whereas he is tired, she is energetic.  ✅ Whereas he is EXHAUSTED, she is full of energy. (stronger contrast)

📖  Grammar — Emphasis structures — What I love is... / It is English that... / The thing that...
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

EMPHASIS STRUCTURES (cleft sentences) highlight one part of a sentence. Type 1: WHAT + S + V + IS: 'What I love IS the colour.' Type 2: IT IS + emphasis + THAT: 'It IS English THAT changed my life.' Type 3: THE THING THAT + clause: 'The thing that strikes me IS the detail.' These structures make simple statements dramatic and emphatic. Common in discussions about art, culture, and personal opinions.

ОНЦЛОН БҮТЭЦ нь өгүүлбэрийн нэг хэсгийг тусгайлан онцолдог. 1: WHAT + S + V + IS: 'What I love IS the colour.' 2: IT IS + онцлол + THAT: 'It IS English THAT changed my life.' 3: THE THING THAT + өгүүлбэр + IS: 'The thing that strikes me IS the detail.' What I love IS... · What matters IS... · It IS English THAT...
The thing THAT strikes me IS... · What I find fascinating IS...
Always: IS/WAS follows the emphasis pattern
What I love IS... (IS нь онцлолын хэсгийн өмнө)
It IS English THAT... (IT IS ... THAT хэлбэр)
The thing THAT strikes me IS... хэлбэр

• What + S + V + IS = emphasise preference
• It is + X + that = emphasise specific element
• The thing that + V + IS = emphasise detail
• Never add extra 'it': What I love IS (not 'it is'!)

📖  Grammar — Future Perfect — will have finished / will have become / by 2030 I will have learned
Learn step by step  ·  Алхам алхмаар судлаарай

The FUTURE PERFECT (will have + past participle) describes an action completed BEFORE a specific future point. 'By 2030, AI WILL HAVE TRANSFORMED every industry.' 'By the time you read this, I WILL HAVE LEFT.' Key signals: by + time (by 2030, by next year, by then), by the time + clause. Compare: Future Simple: 'I will finish.' (at some point) vs Future Perfect: 'I WILL HAVE FINISHED by Friday.' (completed before Friday). Future Perfect emphasises COMPLETION before a deadline.

FUTURE PERFECT (will have + past participle) нь ирээдүйн тодорхой цагаас ӨМНӨ дуусах үйлдлийг тайлбарладаг. 'By 2030, AI WILL HAVE TRANSFORMED every industry.' Гол цагийн илэрхийлэлүүд: by + цаг, by the time + өгүүлбэр. Харьцуул: Future Simple: 'I will finish.' (хэзээ нэгэн цагт) vs Future Perfect: 'I WILL HAVE FINISHED by Friday.' (Баасан гарагаас ӨМНӨ дуусна). Дуусах хугацааг онцолно.
